title: Token-level editing is causal, but content is the weakest action component
decision: Accept the token-aligned interface as operation- and pointer-sensitive, reject pooled metrics for recipe selection, and run local component falsifiers before scale.
plain_summary: Five frozen checkpoints pass a token-level action test on mixed edits. Fresh exposure has no effect; LDAD improves recursive error but weakens action separation. A local audit is needed because content changes affect only a few tokens.
